option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: out solo no funciona aquí. ## READING 1 — MURDER MYSTERY Once, when I was teaching English to children in Cambridge, I died while eating my dinner. The children were eleven to fourteen years old and there were about one hundred and twenty of them in the large hall of a boarding school. We were all eating ice cream when ## I made strange noises and fell forward onto my plate. This was not some unusual way of teaching the children about crime, but a murder mystery game. It was a bit like a play in the theatre, but the whole school was involved. All of the teachers were either victims or suspects and all of the children were detectives trying to find out who the murderer was. The children knew the situation was serious as many teachers were “dying”, so they were all very excited. Lying next to my ice cream, I loved being a “victim”. However, I had to remember not to smile as the teachers dragged me across the floor to get rid of my dead body so the students knew ## I was really “dead”. This was just the start of my murder mystery career! Soon after that, one of my friends bought a murder mystery game and invited me and several other friends to a dinner party. We all had to dress up as different characters. The story was that one of our close friends had been murdered and we were all suspects. We had to tell the other characters important information and question people about the information they gave. Then, at the end of the evening, we tried to work out who had done it. I wasn’t very good at the police investigation part, but ## I loved playing a part and the excitement of knowing that one of the people in the room was a “murderer”. Now I run my own company - Murder Mystery Occasions. I organise murder mystery parties in restaurants or historic buildings. Usually ## I hire a private room so that the “murder” doesn’t upset other guests. Castles are particularly popular with my guests because they’re so atmospheric, so I use them if they’re available. ## I write the scripts and employ actors to be some of the key characters. People pay to come along and join in with the fun while having a wonderful dinner. The murder usually happens somewhere between the starter and the main course and there is always a lot of excitement when the police arrive to take fingerprints and interview key suspects. People tell me that’s the best part. Then, at the end of the evening, there is a prize for the table that guesses who is the murderer, but everybody who comes along seems to enjoy the drama. So, you could say I’ve made a good, honest career out of murder. ##

items.title: Set 03 · Pregunta 28
items.prompt: READING — PREGUNTAS Y SU RESPUESTA ## EXPLICADA # 1. What primarily contributed to the students’ heightened excitement during the event in Cambridge? Answer: According to the text, the students became especially excited because they saw several teachers pretending to die, which made the whole situation feel much more serious and realistic. Explicación de la paráfrasis: Aquí no copiamos literalmente “realistic portrayal of multiple teachers ‘dying’ throughout the game” , sino que lo convertimos en una respuesta más natural: “they saw several teachers pretending to die” . Además, “heightened excitement” pasa a “became especially excited” , que es una forma más simple y más ## B1. La idea clave que debes mantener es: los alumnos se emocionan por el realismo de ver a varios profesores “muriendo” . # 2. In the narrator’s early experience at the dinner-party mystery, what aspect of the game appealed to them the most? Answer: Based on the text, the narrator most enjoyed playing a character and feeling the suspense of having a possible murderer within the group. Explicación de la paráfrasis: La pregunta usa una formulación más compleja: “immersive role-playing and the suspense of being among potential murderers” . La respuesta buena la simplifica a: “playing a character” y “feeling the suspense” . También “among potential murderers” se transforma en “within the group” , que suena más natural y evita copiar. items.context: "Thanks to the witness description and the CCTV images, the police identified the suspect later that night."
items.prompt: What combination of evidence led to the suspect being identified?
items.help_text: Fragmento clave del reading: usa esta pista y elige la paráfrasis correcta.

choices.label: As explained in the text, the identification came from both eyewitness information and security camera images
The suspect confessed at the police station before any witness was interviewed
Only the missing cash connected the suspect to the crime
The victim had taken a clear photo of the thief during the robbery

items.answer: a) As explained in the text, the identification came from both eyewitness information and security camera images

option_explanations.label: As explained in the text, the identification came from both eyewitness information and security camera images
option_explanations.explanation: Correcta: combina descripción de testigos e imágenes de seguridad.

option_explanations.label: The suspect confessed at the police station before any witness was interviewed
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: el texto no menciona confesión.

option_explanations.label: Only the missing cash connected the suspect to the crime
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: la identificación vino de testigos y CCTV, no del dinero desaparecido.

option_explanations.label: The victim had taken a clear photo of the thief during the robbery
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: esa situación no aparece en el reading.

items.explanation_es: Nueva pregunta de paráfrasis centrada en las pruebas que permitieron identificar al sospechoso.

items.title: Reading 1 — Murder Mystery · Pregunta 5
items.context: "All of the teachers were either victims or suspects and all of the children were detectives trying to find out who the murderer was."
items.prompt: How were the teachers and children involved in the school game?
items.help_text: Fragmento clave del reading: usa esta pista y elige la paráfrasis correcta.

choices.label: According to the text, the adults acted as possible criminals or victims, while the students investigated the case
The children performed as suspects and the teachers marked their work from the audience
Only a small group of older students were allowed to join the activity
The game was cancelled because the school hall was too crowded

items.answer: a) According to the text, the adults acted as possible criminals or victims, while the students investigated the case

option_explanations.label: According to the text, the adults acted as possible criminals or victims, while the students investigated the case
option_explanations.explanation: Correcta: resume el reparto de papeles entre profesorado y alumnado.

option_explanations.label: The children performed as suspects and the teachers marked their work from the audience
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: es al revés; los niños eran detectives.

option_explanations.label: Only a small group of older students were allowed to join the activity
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: participó todo el colegio mencionado en el texto.

option_explanations.label: The game was cancelled because the school hall was too crowded
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: el juego sí se llevó a cabo.

items.explanation_es: Nueva pregunta de paráfrasis sobre los papeles de profesores y alumnos.

items.title: Reading 1 — Murder Mystery · Pregunta 6
items.context: "I had to remember not to smile… so the students knew I was really ‘dead’."
items.prompt: Why did the narrator try not to smile while acting as a victim?
items.help_text: Fragmento clave del reading: usa esta pista y elige la paráfrasis correcta.

choices.label: Based on the text, the narrator wanted the scene to look convincing for the students
The narrator was angry about being chosen for the role
The children had been told not to look at the teachers
Smiling would have signalled that the game was already over

items.answer: a) Based on the text, the narrator wanted the scene to look convincing for the students

option_explanations.label: Based on the text, the narrator wanted the scene to look convincing for the students
option_explanations.explanation: Correcta: debía parecer realmente “muerto/a” para mantener el realismo.

option_explanations.label: The narrator was angry about being chosen for the role
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: de hecho dice que le encantaba ser la víctima.

option_explanations.label: The children had been told not to look at the teachers
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: el texto no dice eso.

option_explanations.label: Smiling would have signalled that the game was already over
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: la razón es mantener la credibilidad de la escena.

items.explanation_es: Nueva pregunta de paráfrasis sobre el realismo de la actuación.

items.title: Reading 1 — Murder Mystery · Pregunta 7
items.context: "We had to tell the other characters important information and question people about the information they gave."
items.prompt: What were the guests expected to do during the dinner-party mystery?
items.help_text: Fragmento clave del reading: usa esta pista y elige la paráfrasis correcta.

choices.label: According to the text, they had to share clues and ask each other questions to uncover the case
They only had to watch actors perform the story from their tables
They were required to write a police report before dinner started
They had to vote for the best costume without discussing the crime

items.answer: a) According to the text, they had to share clues and ask each other questions to uncover the case

option_explanations.label: According to the text, they had to share clues and ask each other questions to uncover the case
option_explanations.explanation: Correcta: intercambian información y se interrogan para resolver el misterio.

option_explanations.label: They only had to watch actors perform the story from their tables
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: aquí los invitados participan activamente.

option_explanations.label: They were required to write a police report before dinner started
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: eso no aparece en el reading.

option_explanations.label: They had to vote for the best costume without discussing the crime
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: el foco era resolver quién era el asesino.

items.explanation_es: Nueva pregunta de paráfrasis sobre la dinámica del dinner-party mystery.

items.title: Reading 1 — Murder Mystery · Pregunta 8
items.context: "There is always a lot of excitement when the police arrive to take fingerprints and interview key suspects."
items.prompt: What moment do people reportedly enjoy most at the organised mystery dinners?
items.help_text: Fragmento clave del reading: usa esta pista y elige la paráfrasis correcta.

choices.label: As explained in the text, guests especially enjoy the point when the police investigation begins
People mainly attend because the starter is better than the main course
The favourite part is leaving before the final solution is announced
Guests only enjoy the evening if they personally play the murderer

items.answer: a) As explained in the text, guests especially enjoy the point when the police investigation begins

option_explanations.label: As explained in the text, guests especially enjoy the point when the police investigation begins
option_explanations.explanation: Correcta: el texto dice que ese momento genera mucha emoción y que la gente comenta que es la mejor parte.

option_explanations.label: People mainly attend because the starter is better than the main course
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: la comida no es la idea principal de ese fragmento.

option_explanations.label: The favourite part is leaving before the final solution is announced
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: no se menciona nada parecido.

option_explanations.label: Guests only enjoy the evening if they personally play the murderer
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: el texto indica que todo el mundo parece disfrutar del drama.

items.explanation_es: Nueva pregunta de paráfrasis sobre el momento favorito del evento.
